zh.lifeSummary: 當Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla"於1255年10月23日出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安瓦拉阿多利德時，他的父親Rey Alfonso X de Castilla y León33歲，母親Violante de Aragón19歲。他於1269年11月30日與Blanche de France在西班牙迪奧希斯 德 布爾戈斯結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1275年6月25日在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈希達德 雷亞爾希達德 雷亞爾享年19歲，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安布爾戈斯布爾戈斯布爾戈斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla"于1255年10月23日出生在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安瓦拉阿多利德时，他的父亲Rey Alfonso X de Castilla y León33岁，母亲Violante de Aragón19岁。他于1269年11月30日与Blanche de France在西班牙迪奧希斯 德 布爾戈斯结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1275年6月25日在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈希達德 雷亞爾希達德 雷亞爾享年19岁，埋葬在西班牙卡斯利亞 Y 利安布爾戈斯布爾戈斯布爾戈斯。

nameMeanings.name: Cerda
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Jesus, Francisco, Manuel, Juan, Luis, Ramon, Guadalupe, Ricardo, Miguel, Pedro, Carlos.

Spanish and Portuguese: from cerda ‘bristle, hair’ (from Late Latin cirra), apparently a nickname for someone with a prominent tuft of hair. One of the sons of King Alfonso X (1221–84) was known as Fernando de la Cerda.
Catalan (Cerdà): habitational name, cerdà, for someone from La Cerdanya, a Catalan district in the Pyrenees which is situated partly in Spain and partly in France. Compare Serda .

variants.name: Serda
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Agustin, Angel, Humberto, Juan, Lupe, Manuel, Reynaldo, Santos, Adela, Ana, Angelina, Bacilio.
Catalan:
(Serdà): variant of Cerdà (see Cerda ).

nameMeanings.name: "Infante
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Pedro, Rafael, Manuel, Juan, Angel, Fernando, Ramon, Francisco, Ana, Eduardo.  Italian Antonio, Marco, Angelo, Aniello, Carmine, Leonardo, Adalgisa, Attilio, Carlo, Carmelo, Deno.

Spanish: from infante literally ‘child’ (see 2 below), but in Spain also a title borne by the eldest sons of noblemen before they inherited, and in particular by the son of the king of Castile; thus the surname probably originated either as a nickname for one of a lordly disposition or as an occupational name for a member of the household of an infante. Compare Ynfante .
Italian: nickname for someone with a childlike disposition, from infante ‘child’ (from Latin infans, literally ‘one who cannot speak’).

variants.name: Fant
meanings: English: from Middle English infaunt(e) ‘baby, child’ (Old French enfant), hence a nickname for someone of a childish (or childlike) disposition. This name arose when, in medieval England, Anglo-Norman French l'enfant was wrongly understood as le fant.
Italian (Veneto): variant of   Infante  .

variants.name: Infanti
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Ettore, Marco, Mario, Vittorio.
Italian: patronymic or plural form of   Infante  .

variants.name: Ynfante
meanings: Hispanic: variant of Spanish   Infante  .

variants.name: Infantino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Rocco, Carlo, Carmelina, Carmelo, Carmine, Corrado, Gaspare, Palma.
Italian (mainly Sicily):
from a diminutive of   Infante  .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Castilla"
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Alfredo, Carlos, Raul, Sergio, Jesus, Jorge, Pablo, Ruben, Blanca, Elido, Francisco, Guillermo.
Spanish: habitational name from Castile (Spanish Castilla), a region in central Spain. The name derives from the many castles in the region. This surname is also found in southern France.
History: As an independent kingdom between the 10th and 15th centuries, Castile formed the largest power in the Iberian peninsula.

variants.name: Castiglia
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Salvatore, Angelo, Annamarie, Antonino, Benedetto, Carmine, Francesco, Gaetano, Gennaro, Giacomo, Giuseppe, Ignazio.
Italian: habitational name from Castile (Italian Castiglia) in central Spain (see Castilla ).

nameMeanings.name: Fernando
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A modern form of the old Spanish name Ferdinando, which is of Germanic (Visigothic) origin, from farð ‘journey’ (or possibly an altered form of frið ‘peace’) + nand ‘ready, prepared’. The name was hereditary in the royal families of Spain from an early date. It was borne by Ferdinand I ( d. 1065 ) of Castile and Leon, who conducted successful campaigns against the Moors, and by his descendant Ferdinand V ( 1452–1516 ), who finally expelled the Moors from Spain altogether; he also gave financial backing to Columbus. Through the marriage in 1496 of his daughter Joan the Mad of Castile to the Habsburg Archduke Philip , the name Ferdinand also became hereditary in the Austrian imperial family. Their younger son, Ferdinand ( 1503–64 ), acquired the succession to the kingdoms of Hungary and Bohemia by marriage in 1521 , and became Holy Roman Emperor in 1558 . See Ferdinand .

variants.name: Fernán
meanings: A modern form of the old Spanish name Ferdinando, which is of Germanic (Visigothic) origin, from farð ‘journey’ (or possibly an altered form of frið ‘peace’) + nand ‘ready, prepared’. The name was hereditary in the royal families of Spain from an early date. It was borne by Ferdinand I ( d. 1065 ) of Castile and Leon, who conducted successful campaigns against the Moors, and by his descendant Ferdinand V ( 1452–1516 ), who finally expelled the Moors from Spain altogether; he also gave financial backing to Columbus. Through the marriage in 1496 of his daughter Joan the Mad of Castile to the Habsburg Archduke Philip , the name Ferdinand also became hereditary in the Austrian imperial family. Their younger son, Ferdinand ( 1503–64 ), acquired the succession to the kingdoms of Hungary and Bohemia by marriage in 1521 , and became Holy Roman Emperor in 1558 . See Ferdinand .

variants.name: Hernán
meanings: Variant of   Fernando .

variants.name: Hernando
meanings: Variant of   Fernando .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of any of the given names beginning with the letter D- ( compare Jay and Kay ), in particular Dorothy . It is also used as an independent name, and may in some cases be associated with the River Dee ( compare Clyde ).

nameMeanings.name: la
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: ‘Grace’.

en.lifeSummary: When Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla" was born on 23 October 1255, in Valladolid, Castilla y León, Spain, his father, Rey Alfonso X de Castilla y León, was 33 and his mother, Violante de Aragón, was 19. He married Blanche de France on 30 November 1269, in Diócesis de Burgos, Spain. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died on 25 June 1275, in Ciudad Real, Ciudad Real, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain, at the age of 19, and was buried in Burgos, Burgos, Burgos, Castilla y León, Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla" est né le 23 octobre 1255, à Valladolid, Castilla y León, Espagne, son père, Rey Alfonso X de Castilla y León, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Violante de Aragón, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Blanche de France le 30 novembre 1269, à Diócesis de Burgos, Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé le 25 juin 1275, à Ciudad Real, Ciudad Real, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne, à l’âge de 19 ans, et a été enterré à Burgos, Burgos, Burgos, Castilla y León, Espagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla" am 23. Oktober 1255 in Valladolid, Castilla y León, Spanien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Rey Alfonso X de Castilla y León 33 und seine Mutter Violante de Aragón 19. Er hat am 30. November 1269 in Diócesis de Burgos, Spanien Blanche de France geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb am 25. Juni 1275 im Alter von 19 Jahren in Ciudad Real, Ciudad Real, Castilla-La Mancha, Spanien und wurde in Burgos, Burgos, Burgos, Castilla y León, Spanien begraben.

ja.lifeSummary: Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla"が1255年10月23日にスペインカスティラ イ レoンヴァラドリドで生まれた時，父Rey Alfonso X de Castilla y Leónは33歳，母Violante de Aragónは19歳であった。1269年11月30日にスペインディoセシス ド ブルゴスでBlanche de Franceと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1275年6月25日にスペインカスティラ-ラ マンチャシウダド レアルシウダド レアルで19歳で死去し，スペインカスティラ イ レoンブルゴスブルゴスブルゴスにて埋葬された。

ko.Ciudad Real, Ciudad Real, Castilla-La Ma: 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 시우다드 리 시우다드 리
ko.lifeSummary: Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla"(이)가 1255년 10월 23일 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 발라도리드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Rey Alfonso X de Castilla y León(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Violante de Aragón(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1269년 11월 30일 스페인 디o스시스 데 버고스에서 Blanche de France(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1275년 6월 25일 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 시우다드 리 시우다드 리에서 19세의 나이로 사망했으며 스페인 카스틸라 이 레o 버고스 버고스 버고스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla" nasceu em 23 de outubro de 1255, em Valladolid, Castilla y León, Espanha, seu pai, Rey Alfonso X de Castilla y León, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Violante de Aragón, tinha 19 anos. Ele casou-se com Blanche de France em 30 de novembro de 1269, em Diócesis de Burgos, Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu em 25 de junho de 1275, em Ciudad Real, Ciudad Real, Castilla-La Mancha, Espanha, com 19 anos, e foi sepultado em Burgos, Burgos, Burgos, Castilla y León, Espanha.

ru.Ciudad Real, Ciudad Real, Castilla-La Ma: сюдад риль, сюдад риль, кастилла-ла манча, Испания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla" родился 23 октября 1255 г. в местности валладолид, кастилла и леoн, Испания, его отцу по имени Rey Alfonso X de Castilla y León было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Violante de Aragón, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Blanche de France 30 ноября 1269 г. в местности диoсесис де бургос, Испания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер 25 июня 1275 г. в местности сюдад риль, сюдад риль, кастилла-ла манча, Испания в возрасте 19 лет и был похоронен в местности бургос, бургос, бургос, кастилла и леoн, Испания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Fernando de la Cerda "Infante de Castilla" nació el 23 de octubre de 1255, en Valladolid, Castilla y León, España, su papá, Rey Alfonso X de Castilla y León, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Violante de Aragón, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Blanche de France el 30 de noviembre de 1269, en Diócesis de Burgos, España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió el 25 de junio de 1275, en Ciudad Real, Ciudad Real, Castilla-La Mancha, España, a la edad de 19 años, y fue sepultado en Burgos, Burgos, Burgos, Castilla y León, España.
